pi_items.source: export_result.sh explicitly checks for manager@company.com in to_addr
export_result.sh evaluates the F flag in Maildir filenames

export_result.sh uses subject.lower().strip().startswith("re:") which strictly requires the subject to start with "re:". This explicitly contradicts the claimed case-insensitive regex "(?i)re:" which allows "re:" anywhere in the subject.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on 50 real emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus. The setup script explicitly modifies their subjects to create a 50/50 split of replies and new topics. The expected archive folder name "Replies-Archive" and the recipient "manager@company.com" are verified to match the export_result.sh evaluation logic. The required flag corresponds to the "F" Maildir flag. A significant contradiction was found regarding the subject pattern: while the task instructions and metadata claim the pattern is "(?i)re:" (meaning "contains Re:"), the evaluation script strictly requires the subject to *start* with "Re:" after stripping whitespace (i.e., "^(?i)\\s*re:"). Consequently, an email with the subject "[zzzzteana] RE: Alexander" will be incorrectly penalized as a false positive if moved to the archive.
